Package com.sbdc.loggerhead

Class Table

java.lang.Object
com.sbdc.loggerhead.Table
public class Table extends Object
Way of structuring loggers other than manually building paths for loggers. Each table provides methods to get subtables and add loggers to them. It should be noted that no Table actually maintains references to any logger. Calls to add*Logger methods simply reference methods on Loggerhead

    • Table

      Parameters:

      loggerhead - Loggerhead reference
      parent - parent table

  • Method Details

    • hasRoot

      public static boolean hasRoot()
      Returns:
      True if the root table has been set

    • applyToSubTable

      public Table applyToSubTable(String newTableName, Consumer<Table> applyToTable)
      Creates subtable table or gets an existing subtable, then applies a callable to it
      Parameters:
      newTableName - Subtable name
      applyToTable - Callable accepting the Subtable
      Returns:
      The parent table for chaining

    • getSubTable

      public Table getSubTable(String newTableName)
      Creates subtable with name, or gets the existing subtable with that name
      Parameters:
      newTableName - Subtable's name
      Returns:
      Subtable

    • getSubTables

      public HashMap<String,Table> getSubTables()
      Returns:
      HashMap mapping all subtable names to thier subtables

    • getParent

      public Table getParent()
      Returns:
      This tables parent. On a root table this will loop back to itself

    • clearSubtables

      public void clearSubtables()
      Clears all subtables from this tables

    • addStringLogger

      public Table addStringLogger(String key, LogMode mode, Supplier<String> stringGetter)
      Add a string logger to the Loggerhead instance, prefixed with this tables full path
      Parameters:
      key - Name of the string logger without slashes
      mode - Logging mode for the string logger
      stringGetter - Callable providing the string
      Returns:
      This table for chaining

    • addDoubleLogger

      public Table addDoubleLogger(String key, LogMode mode, Supplier<Double> doubleGetter)
      Add a double logger to the Loggerhead instance, prefixed with this tables full path
      Parameters:
      key - Name of the double logger without slashes
      mode - Logging mode for the string logger
      doubleGetter - Callable providing the double
      Returns:
      This table for chaining

      public Table addIntegerLogger(String key, LogMode mode, Supplier<Integer> integerGetter)

      Parameters:
      key - Name of the integer logger without slashes
      mode - Logging mode for the integer logger
      integerGetter - Callable providing the integer
      Returns:
      This table for chaining

      public Table addBooleanLogger(String key, LogMode mode, Supplier<Boolean> boolGetter)
      Add a boolean logger to the Loggerhead instance, prefixed with this tables full path
      Parameters:

      mode - Logging mode for the boolean logger
      boolGetter - Callable providing the boolean
      Returns:
      This table for chaining

      public Table addSwerveStateLogger(String key, LogMode mode, Supplier<SwerveModuleState[]> moduleStateGetter)
      Add a swerve state logger to the Loggerhead instance, prefixed with this tables full path
      Parameters:
      key - Name of the swerve state logger without slashes
      mode - Logging mode for the swerve state logger
      moduleStateGetter - Callable providing the swerve state
      Returns:
      This table for chaining

    • addCompoundLogger

      public Table addCompoundLogger(CompoundLogger compoundLogger)
      Add a compound logger to the Loggerhead instance. The compound logger is responsible for placing child loggers. See CompoundLogger.initialize(String)
      Parameters:
      compoundLogger - The compound logger
      Returns:
      This table for chaining

      public Table addLoggable(Loggable loggable, LogMode logMode)
